title: Labor Pains

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening scene establishing Homer's frustration
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation of annoyance
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly economical and character-specific but not particularly creative in this context

jokes.text: Did you know some historians think American football is based on an ancient Aztec game? / It's not American football, it's just football.
jokes.setup: Lisa shares historical trivia about American football
jokes.punchline: British character corrects the terminology, revealing cultural perspective
jokes.explanation: Nice cultural joke playing on American vs. British terminology for football/soccer

jokes.text: Mom, Lisa's ruining football. American football.
jokes.setup: Bart complains about Lisa, then corrects himself
jokes.punchline: Immediate callback to the football terminology joke
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that shows the football terminology confusion spreading

jokes.text: Money doesn't grow on trees. It grows on threes.
jokes.setup: Homer starts with common saying about money not growing on trees
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's gambling, money grows on 'threes' (cards)
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that subverts the familiar saying while revealing Homer's gambling habit. Very economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: I should not have anted with my blood.
jokes.setup: Players are folding because stakes are too high
jokes.punchline: One player reveals he literally bet his blood
jokes.explanation: Darkly absurd escalation of poker stakes that's both shocking and funny

jokes.text: Hey, you gotta pay to find out.
jokes.setup: Someone asks 'What would Jesus do?' about Homer's gambling
jokes.punchline: Homer treats it like a poker bet requiring payment to see the answer
jokes.explanation: Clever application of poker logic to a moral question, very Homer-like

jokes.text: Who's president now, Alexander Hamilton?
jokes.setup: Player seems confused about current events
jokes.punchline: Confuses historical figure Hamilton with being president
jokes.explanation: Mild historical confusion joke, somewhat generic

jokes.text: Everyone has a cell phone. Mine's at home.
jokes.setup: Pregnant woman asks if Homer has a cell phone
jokes.punchline: Homer states everyone has one, then immediately contradicts himself
jokes.explanation: Nice Homer logic fail - states universal truth then immediately proves himself wrong

jokes.text: This reminds me of that scene in Alien. / The scene where she escapes with the cat at the end.
jokes.setup: Homer compares childbirth to Alien movie
jokes.punchline: References the peaceful ending instead of the violent chest-burster scene
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - sets up expectation of chest-burster scene but goes with sweet ending

jokes.text: You like lies? Here's a few: College is expensive but it's worth it.
jokes.setup: Woman thanks Homer for lying about her boyfriend being there
jokes.punchline: Homer offers cynical 'lie' about college value
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about societal lies, fits Homer's cynical side

jokes.text: Your baby doesn't have any legs. Oh, wait, there's more.
jokes.setup: Homer announces alarming birth defect
jokes.punchline: Realizes he was looking at the wrong end
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on Homer's incompetence as impromptu midwife

jokes.text: It's a dude. And he's uncut. Very Euro.
jokes.setup: Homer announces baby's gender
jokes.punchline: Casually observes circumcision status with cultural commentary
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate but matter-of-fact observation with cultural stereotype

jokes.text: For unto you is born this day, in this elevator, inspected in August 2009, a baby.
jokes.setup: Homer does biblical birth announcement
jokes.punchline: Includes mundane elevator inspection date in the proclamation
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of sacred biblical language with bureaucratic mundanity

jokes.text: That guy didn't even look over once. / Lobby please.
jokes.setup: Homer comments on other elevator passenger's indifference
jokes.punchline: Other passenger casually requests lobby floor after witnessing birth
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of ultimate urban indifference to dramatic events

jokes.text: You can hear the players swear from up here. / I'll kick your ass, Milhouse.
jokes.setup: Comment about hearing players swear from good seats
jokes.punchline: Milhouse immediately demonstrates with his own swearing
jokes.explanation: Nice immediate demonstration of the setup, though fairly predictable

jokes.text: Don't call me 'Dad.' If people think peanut vendors have families, it's too sad.
jokes.setup: Child calls peanut vendor 'Dad'
jokes.punchline: Vendor explains the existential sadness of his profession having families
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly dark and philosophical observation about working-class dignity

jokes.text: I'm kind of pessimistic about the team's chances. They're gonna get creamed.
jokes.setup: Lisa is picked to be junior cheerleader
jokes.punchline: She immediately expresses pessimism instead of enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - intellectually honest even in cheerleading context

jokes.text: I'm dating a cheerleader. / No, you're not. / Burned again, Milhouse.
jokes.setup: Milhouse brags about dating a cheerleader
jokes.punchline: Nelson immediately shuts him down with signature 'burned' comment
jokes.explanation: Classic Nelson bullying dynamic, well-executed but predictable

jokes.text: Now here's your 50 bucks each which should just about cover your parking.
jokes.setup: Coach pays cheerleaders for their performance
jokes.punchline: Payment barely covers parking costs
jokes.explanation: Realistic observation about exploitation in entertainment industry

jokes.text: After you wear that, you need to be hosed down for bedbugs.
jokes.setup: Discussion about mascot costume punishment
jokes.punchline: Reveals disgusting hygiene conditions of mascot costume
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor about working conditions, fairly standard

jokes.text: So now there's two things named after me-- a baby, and a law banning air horns after 3:00 a.m.
jokes.setup: Homer is honored to have baby named after him
jokes.punchline: Reveals he also has a noise ordinance named after him
jokes.explanation: Perfect character joke revealing Homer's history of antisocial behavior

jokes.text: The novels of Charlotte Bronte. / Um, I thought we were playing cards.
jokes.setup: Someone says 'read 'em and weep'
jokes.punchline: Person literally has novels instead of cards
jokes.explanation: Literal interpretation of poker expression creates mild confusion comedy

jokes.text: No, you can't leave. I'm dealin' you in.
jokes.setup: Homer offers to babysit but says he has plans
jokes.punchline: New mother desperately forces him into her card game
jokes.explanation: Relatable new parent desperation, forcing social interaction

jokes.text: Kids blow up so fast.
jokes.setup: Montage of baby growing up quickly
jokes.punchline: Homer says 'blow up' instead of 'grow up'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism, characteristically mixing up common expressions

jokes.text: This simulation has been brought to you by your brain, a subsidiary of your penis.
jokes.setup: Homer's fantasy scenario is revealed as mental simulation
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on male decision-making hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about male psychology with perfect corporate parody structure

jokes.text: First few weeks of what? / Um, November.
jokes.setup: Homer mentions being tired from 'first few weeks'
jokes.punchline: Desperately claims he meant the month of November
jokes.explanation: Good example of Homer's terrible lying skills and panic responses

jokes.text: You been hit by the cheerleading bug, little lady? / Actually, I've been hit by the unfair labor practices bug.
jokes.setup: Coach asks if Lisa caught cheerleading enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Lisa turns it into labor organizing terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - turns cutesy language into serious social justice terminology

jokes.text: We're gonna push 'em back, push 'em back, push 'em way, way back. All the way to a new contract. 'Cause union power is a fact.
jokes.setup: Lisa leading cheerleaders in organizing
jokes.punchline: Labor chant disguised as cheerleading routine
jokes.explanation: Clever fusion of cheerleading and labor organizing, perfect Lisa character moment

jokes.text: How? By size, brand, value? And don't even suggest race, 'cause I don't see that. I just see little people I own.
jokes.setup: Coach suggests Lisa organize her dollies
jokes.punchline: Lisa overthinks organization methods then makes dark ownership comment
jokes.explanation: Lisa's analytical mind applied to children's toys with darkly funny ownership revelation

jokes.text: Not right, not fair. Let's say it with our hair.
jokes.setup: Cheerleaders expressing solidarity with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Their distinctly cheerleader-style protest method
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing how cheerleaders would naturally protest

jokes.text: My vest isn't the only thing that's ripped.
jokes.setup: Coach appears at cheerleading practice
jokes.punchline: Makes inappropriate comment about his physique to young cheerleaders
jokes.explanation: Generic sleazy coach joke, relies on inappropriate behavior for humor

jokes.text: I'm just teaching myself massage here with online videos and a store mannequin.
jokes.setup: Marge apologizes for bursting in looking for Homer
jokes.punchline: Moe explains his creepy solo massage practice
jokes.explanation: Typical Moe creepiness, well-executed character moment but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Tell me what a cow says. / Aah! My other baby mama.
jokes.setup: Homer teaching baby to make animal sounds
jokes.punchline: Marge's arrival causes Homer to scream in panic
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from innocent baby play to adultery panic

jokes.text: I'm a Schwarzenegger wife. But you're also the housekeeper, so it's all good.
jokes.setup: Gretchen compares herself to Arnold Schwarzenegger's situation
jokes.punchline: References the specific scandal details matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Timely reference to celebrity scandal with dark humor about power dynamics

jokes.text: I haven't had a drink for a week. / That is pretty good.
jokes.setup: Gretchen says nothing is good in her situation
jokes.punchline: Marge finds something positive in week of sobriety
jokes.explanation: Marge's optimistic nature finding silver lining in addiction struggles

jokes.text: And the really funny thing is I'm a doctor.
jokes.setup: Homer tells story of delivering baby in elevator
jokes.punchline: Other passenger reveals he was a doctor the whole time
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback revealing the indifferent elevator passenger was actually qualified to help

jokes.text: I thought that was strange poop on your collar.
jokes.setup: Homer has been spending time with baby
jokes.punchline: Marge noticed baby evidence but misidentified it
jokes.explanation: Basic parenting humor about mysterious stains, fairly standard

jokes.text: Even though the mortgage is due on the first, you can usually wait till the 17th.
jokes.setup: Homer gives fatherly advice to baby
jokes.punchline: Shares practical but concerning financial tip
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's questionable but experienced life advice

jokes.text: You always said charity begins at home. But not which home.
jokes.setup: Homer justifies visiting other woman's baby
jokes.punchline: Exploits loophole in common saying about charity
jokes.explanation: Clever manipulation of familiar saying to justify bad behavior, very Homer-like logic

jokes.text: It's the toes that get you, Marge. It's the toes.
jokes.setup: Homer claims exhaustion from 'Head, Shoulders, Knees and Toes'
jokes.punchline: Identifies toes as the most challenging part
jokes.explanation: Homer treating children's song like serious physical challenge

jokes.text: You're the greatest generation, worthy of our veneration. Go Grampa! / If this is heaven, why don't my shoes match?
jokes.setup: Cheerleaders perform routine for elderly man
jokes.punchline: Grampa's confused response about mismatched shoes in heaven
jokes.explanation: Classic Grampa confusion about reality, characteristic elderly perspective

jokes.text: I'm gonna do what we secretly did at the Alamo-- surrender.
jokes.setup: Coach faces union pressure from cheerleaders
jokes.punchline: Reveals historically inaccurate 'secret' about the Alamo
jokes.explanation: Subversive historical 'fact' used to justify giving up, mildly clever

jokes.text: You can't even kiss me good night without slicing me with your stubble. / Why you little... I'll give you a good-night kiss you'll never forget.
jokes.setup: Bart complains about Homer's rough stubble
jokes.punchline: Homer responds with threats of more aggressive affection
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer-Bart physical comedy escalation, familiar dynamic

jokes.text: I have a soul and you guys don't.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to out-monkey the monkeys
jokes.punchline: Claims spiritual superiority while acting like an animal
jokes.explanation: Ironic claim of spiritual superiority while engaging in primitive behavior

jokes.text: If they get her in their warrens, we'll never see her again.
jokes.setup: Baby Maggie rolls toward prairie dog exhibit
jokes.punchline: Bart treats prairie dogs like serious threat with underground networks
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation treating small animals as major threat with complex infrastructure

jokes.text: The sky is blue, but nobody really knows why. Don't believe what they tell you.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to impart fatherly wisdom quickly
jokes.punchline: Gives conspiracy-tinged advice about basic science
jokes.explanation: Homer's questionable wisdom mixing basic observations with paranoia

jokes.text: One day I'll be that guy who comes to your wedding, makes you feel a little weird, and then runs away.
jokes.setup: Homer imagines future relationship with baby
jokes.punchline: Accurately predicts his own awkward future behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware character moment with realistic prediction of awkward future scenario

jokes.text: You didn't tell me you were pregnant. / I didn't want you to marry me because I was pregnant. I wanted you to marry me because I was nagging you.
jokes.setup: Typical pregnancy relationship discussion
jokes.punchline: Gretchen's preference for nagging as motivation
jokes.explanation: Sharp relationship humor about marriage motivations, good structure and character voice

jokes.text: I wanted you to marry me because I was nagging you.
jokes.setup: Gretchen explains she didn't want pregnancy-based marriage
jokes.punchline: Prefers relationship based on constant nagging
jokes.explanation: Self-aware relationship humor about traditional gender dynamics

jokes.text: Dude, he's my kid. He likes what I like. / But n-n-n-nurture.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to give parenting advice about baby's preferences
jokes.punchline: Homer desperately appeals to nature vs. nurture debate
jokes.explanation: Homer's awkward attempt to maintain relevance through psychology concepts

jokes.text: Really? Then this is much creepier than I thought. / Much.
jokes.setup: Homer learns Chase already has kids of his own
jokes.punchline: Both acknowledge the awkwardness of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good acknowledgment of the uncomfortable implications of Homer's attachment

jokes.text: If you could talk, I know you'd have a few choice words for me.
jokes.setup: Homer addresses Maggie after neglecting her
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges baby would be justifiably angry if she could speak
jokes.explanation: Standard parent guilt humor, Homer recognizing his poor priorities

jokes.text: There were cheerleaders in our house and nobody told me?
jokes.setup: Homer sees evidence of cheerleader visit
jokes.punchline: Focuses on missing cheerleaders rather than union organizing
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer priorities - completely misses the important story for the superficial aspect

tmdb_overview: When Homer helps a woman give birth in an elevator, he forms an emotional attachment to the baby; cheerleaders for the local professional football team recruit Lisa.
